name: Brian Robinson
headline: From feature back to forgotten man
body:
The 2025 collapse is hard to overstate: Robinson cratered to RB56 on just 92 carries across 17 games, his snap share gutted to 16.7% after a midseason move. The 400 rushing yards and two scores barely cleared 54 fantasy points, and a minus-0.046 EPA per play confirmed he was a passenger, not a driver. Even his efficiency markers thinned, with 1.77 yards after contact per attempt and a 4.35-yard average that flattered light volume.

Rewind only one year and the contrast stings. In 2024 he was an RB27 workhorse on 187 carries, 51.6% of snaps, eight touchdowns and 46 red-zone looks. That early-down, goal-line profile still exists, but his receiving role has shriveled from a 1.36 YPRR peak in 2023 to 0.37 last season. He needs a clear path to opportunity, and right now Atlanta's backfield does not promise one. Treat him as a late-round bench dart.
